novel_features.name: Novelty diffing
novel_features.description: Run an Actor and emit only items not seen in prior runs of that Actor.
novel_features.rationale: Local SQLite of prior dataset items lets us URL+content-hash diff every run; no API or other tool has memory of prior runs.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when you need 'what's new since last run' across recurring scrapes for a newsletter or alert workflow.

novel_features.name: Cross-actor dataset FTS
novel_features.description: Full-text search across every cached dataset from every Actor, normalized into a common schema.
novel_features.rationale: SQLite FTS5 over a normalized cross-Actor schema in the local store; Apify offers no cross-dataset search and the official CLI fetches one dataset at a time.
novel_features.example: apify-pp search "model context protocol" --since 7d --actors twitter,reddit,hn --json --select url,title,source_actor
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when you want 'what did anyone say about X this week' without juggling 8 dataset IDs.

novel_features.name: True cost ledger
novel_features.description: Per-run USD cost report joining cached run metadata with the Apify monthly usage endpoint.
novel_features.rationale: Joins runs with users/me/usage to expose per-run cost (CU + RAM-hours + storage + PPE); the dashboard only shows aggregates and the API exposes raw numbers without rollup.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this whenever the user asks 'why is my Apify bill so high' or 'which Actor costs most per useful item.'

novel_features.name: Newsletter digest renderer
novel_features.description: Render a markdown digest from local store: dedupe by URL + title-similarity, rank by engagement+recency+novelty, fill a Go template.
novel_features.rationale: Requires the local normalized FTS store plus novelty + dedupe; nothing in the Apify stack renders markdown digests.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this as the last step of any newsletter / weekly report workflow; output is ready to paste into Beehiiv or pipe to a writer agent.

novel_features.name: Curated weekly workflow
novel_features.description: Run a YAML-declared workflow that chains run → normalize → novelty → digest → publish across multiple Actors.
novel_features.rationale: Replaces a 200-line bash script with one declarative file Claude or another agent can invoke as a single MCP tool.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when the user wants a recurring 'fire one command, get the newsletter' pipeline rather than orchestrating Actor calls by hand.

novel_features.name: Schedule-as-code
novel_features.description: Declarative cron + Actor input bundle with terraform-style plan/apply/diff against the live Apify schedule API.
novel_features.rationale: Dashboard-managed schedules drift; YAML in git is auditable and the Apify schedule API has no first-party GitOps story.
novel_features.example: apify-pp schedule apply ./schedules.yaml --dry-run && apify-pp schedule diff
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user manages 3+ schedules and wants them version-controlled.

novel_features.name: Cost budget enforcement
novel_features.description: Pre-flight cost projection from local p50/p90 of past runs for the same Actor; aborts run if projection exceeds budget.
novel_features.rationale: Apify has org-level limits but no per-invocation guard; only feasible because we have local run history.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this on any long-tail or untrusted Actor to fail-closed on cost rather than learning about it on the invoice.

novel_features.name: Input presets
novel_features.description: Capture known-good Actor input JSON from a prior run and replay with overrides.
novel_features.rationale: Apify's input templates live in Actor READMEs, not in the user's workflow; we persist them locally.
novel_features.example: apify-pp preset save twitter weekly-ai --from-run abc123 && apify-pp call twitter --preset weekly-ai --override startUrls=@new-list.txt
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to stop hand-rebuilding input JSON every time you re-run a recurring scrape.

novel_features.name: Actor A/B cost+quality test
novel_features.description: Run the same input through two competing Actors, normalize via unified schema, report cost-per-novel-item and overlap percentage.
novel_features.rationale: Impossible without the local normalize + novelty layers; nothing else lets you compare two scrapers head-to-head.
novel_features.example: apify-pp ab run apidojo/tweet-scraper kaitoeasyapi/twitter-x-data-tweet-scraper-pay-per-result-cheapest --input shared.json --judge novelty --json
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this whenever the user wonders 'is the cheaper Actor good enough' before committing to a recurring schedule.

novel_features.name: Local replay mode
novel_features.description: Run queries and template renders against the local SQLite copy of past datasets without hitting the API.
novel_features.rationale: Apify charges for every dataset read; we already cached the items, so further iteration is free.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this while tuning templates or dedupe heuristics so iteration is free.

narrative.headline: Every Apify platform feature, plus a local SQLite store, cross-Actor search, novelty diffing, cost-aware runs, and newsletter-ready digests no other Apify tool offers.
narrative.value_prop: apify-pp-cli is the operator CLI for the Apify platform — agent-native JSON across every endpoint, plus a layer of features built on top of a local store that the official CLI, SDKs, and MCP can't reach: cross-Actor full-text search (`search`), novel-only runs (`run --only-new`), per-run cost ledger (`cost report`), and templated newsletter digests (`digest`).
narrative.auth_narrative: Set `APIFY_TOKEN` (from Apify Console → Settings → Integrations) or run `apify-pp auth set-token`. The CLI validates the token against `/v2/users/me` before any call. The query-param `?token=` form is supported but discouraged.

quickstart.comment: Verify token, API reachability, and quota status.
Browse the public Actor store (no auth required).
Run an Actor and block until terminal state, with cost projection.
Run an Actor and emit only items not seen in prior runs.
Full-text search across every cached dataset item, normalized.
Render a newsletter-ready markdown digest from the local store.

troubleshoots.fix: Re-run `apify-pp auth set-token` with a fresh token from Apify Console → Settings → Integrations.
Check `apify-pp runs log <id> --follow`; abort with `apify-pp runs abort <id>` if the Actor is hung.
Run `apify-pp sync --runs --since 30d` to backfill local run metadata first.
No prior runs for that Actor are in the local store yet; the second run onward will dedupe.
Someone edited the schedule in the dashboard; reconcile with `apify-pp schedule pull > schedules.yaml`.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for apify-pp-cli when you need to orchestrate multiple Apify Actors as part of a recurring research, scraping, or newsletter pipeline. It's the right tool when you want per-run cost visibility, novel-only output, cross-Actor search, or schedule-as-code. For Actor development (writing your own scrapers), use the official `apify-cli`; this CLI is for platform operators.

recipes.title: Weekly newsletter scrape
recipes.explanation: One YAML-declared chain across Twitter + Reddit + HN + News scrapers; emits a single newsletter-ready digest.

recipes.title: Cost-capped exploratory run
recipes.explanation: Pre-flight projection from local history; aborts before start if projected cost exceeds budget.

recipes.title: Cross-Actor topic search (compact)
recipes.explanation: FTS5 over the local store; use `--select` with dotted paths to avoid pulling the full normalized item payload.

recipes.title: Schedule drift check
recipes.explanation: Terraform-style diff between local YAML and the live Apify schedules; output is a JSON change set.

recipes.title: A/B test two Twitter scrapers
recipes.command: apify-pp-cli ab run apidojo/tweet-scraper kaitoeasyapi/twitter-x-data-tweet-scraper --input @shared.json --judge cost-per-novel --agent
recipes.explanation: Runs both, normalizes via unified schema, reports cost-per-novel-item and overlap %.

trigger_phrases: what's new in my reddit scraper
build a newsletter from this week's scrapes
how much did my apify scrapes cost this month
